title: The sections that matter
content:
A CV is a few standard blocks. Include these, in roughly this order:

- **Contact** - name, professional title, email, phone, city/country, LinkedIn URL. Use a professional email. No full street address, no date of birth, and in most markets (US/UK especially) **no photo** - it can trigger ATS and bias issues.
- **Summary / headline** - 2-3 lines stating who you are professionally and what you're strong at. Skip the old "Objective" ("seeking a role where I can grow"). Recruiters want value, not wishes.
- **Experience** - the core of the CV. Covered in the next lesson.
- **Education** - degree, institution, year. Put it above experience only if you're a student or recent grad with little work history; otherwise it goes below.
- **Skills** - a clean list of hard skills and tools, plus a few relevant soft skills. This section is also where ATS keywords live.
- **Optional, if relevant:** Certifications, Projects, Awards, Languages, Volunteer work, Publications. Add them only if they strengthen your case for *this* job.

Keep section headings standard ("Experience", "Education", "Skills"). Creative headings confuse ATS.

---
